stem: A candidate enters the hiring pipeline. The probability of passing resume screening is 96%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing phone interview is 74%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing onsite interview is between 61% and 71% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). What is the overall probability that the candidate is hired?

ground_truth.answer: P(hired) ranges from 0.433 to 0.504 as the onsite interview pass rate varies from 61% to 71%. Point estimate: 0.469.

solution_steps: The candidate must pass all 3 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3).
The uncertainty in the onsite interview stage creates a 7.1 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the onsite interview rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
